data.title: Freddie Mac assumed at Outperform as Wedbush awaits potential IPO
data.description:
Freddie Mac (FMCC) shares were assumed at Outperform as Wedbush analyst Henry Coffey awaits possible action from the Trump administration to recapitalize the mortgage giant, "put it on the path to leaving conservatorship, and monetize Treasury’s investments in the company."

U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick said last week he would like to see something get done by Q1 2026. "Big Short" investor Michael Burry has contended that a relisting is "nearly upon us." Meanwhile, Bill Ackman has said that Fannie Mae (FNMA) and Freddie Mac (FMCC) were still a long way from being ready to IPO, though he offered several ways to potentially accelerate the process, such as the U.S. Treasury exercising its 79.9% warrants in both companies.

Coffey laid out the immediate risk to his investment view: "the uncertainty about exactly what course the Administration is likely to pursue to monetize its investment in FNMA and ultimately recap and release (from conservatorship) both government-sponsored entities (GSEs, Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac)."

He sees "reasonable" chances that the administration "will proceed in a manner that does not put current share value at risk and that raises the capital needed for FNMA to exit conservatorship and, to transfer mortgage risk back to the private sector over time," the note said.

Wedbush's Outperform rate compares with the SA Quant system rating of Strong Buy, as well as the average SA analyst rating of Buy and the average Wall Street analyst rating of Hold.

FMCC shares were down 7% in Friday afternoon trading.

data.title: Zedge signals robust subscription growth and DataSeeds momentum while advancing innovation pipeline
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Zedge, Inc. (ZDGE) Q1 2026

Management View

  • Jonathan Reich, CEO & President, emphasized, "We delivered a solid start to fiscal 2026 with a return to mid-single-digit revenue growth and continued progress across our strategic priorities. What stands out to me this quarter is the quality of that growth for the Zedge marketplace. Subscriptions remained a powerful engine with active subscriptions reaching a record 1.1 million, up more than 50% year-over-year." He highlighted that average revenue per monthly active user increased, and the company was able to offset declines at Emojipedia and GuruShots with strength elsewhere in the ecosystem.

  • Reich detailed progress for DataSeeds, noting, "Recently, we received a second order from an existing customer, a leader in the AI sector. The new order size was approximately 25x the value of the original order placed in Q4 FY '25." He described the DataSeeds Production Cloud as a "managed global network of professional photographers, videographers and graphic artists who produce high-quality, purpose-built datasets on demand."

  • On Tapedeck, Reich stated the pilot was progressing with positive early artist feedback, and the next steps focus on catalog expansion and listener engagement. He outlined a disciplined approach to scaling Tapedeck, ensuring alignment with financial return objectives.

  • Reich addressed free cash flow, explaining, "Free cash flow this quarter reflected the timing of restructuring-related compensation tax payments, which have now largely been completed. We also reinvested part of our restructuring savings into high potential initiatives including DataSeeds, Tapedeck and our innovation pipeline."

  • Reich reported that during the quarter, the company "repurchased 240,000 Class B shares and paid our first quarterly dividend, which reflects our confidence in the long-term value creation potential of the company."

  • Yi Tsai, CFO & Treasurer, stated, "Total revenue for the first quarter was $7.6 million, up 5.8% from last year. Zedge Marketplace revenue was up nicely from last year, driven by strong advertising CPMs and subscription revenues."

Outlook

  • Reich expressed confidence, stating, "With the foundation we have in place, we believe fiscal 2026 will be a year defined by growth, innovation and expanding operating leverage."

  • Tsai noted, "We expect to begin lapping some of these weaker GuruShots comparisons in fiscal 2026," signaling a possible improvement in year-over-year results for that segment.

Financial Results

  • Tsai reported, "Total revenue for the first quarter was $7.6 million, up 5.8% from last year."

  • Advertising revenue was up 6% for the quarter. Zedge Plus subscription revenue increased 29% year-over-year, with the subscriber base growing 54% to nearly 1.1 million.

  • Deferred revenue reached $5.7 million, up 7% sequentially and 55% year-over-year.

  • Cost of revenue was 7.3%, up from 6.4% last year, attributed to reductions in Google Cloud Services discounts and the introduction of Tapedeck licensing fees and DataSeeds production costs.

  • SG&A decreased about 13% to $5.9 million for the quarter, reflecting restructuring savings partially offset by investment in DataSeeds and Tapedeck.

  • GAAP income from operations was $0.9 million compared to a loss of $0.5 million last year, and GAAP net income and EPS was $0.8 million and $0.06 compared to losses of $0.3 million and negative $0.02 last year, respectively.

  • Cash flow from operations was $0.8 million and free cash flow was $0.6 million. Free cash flow was impacted by approximately $350,000 in tax withholding payments related to restructuring.

  • Adjusted EBITDA for the quarter was $1.2 million versus $0.3 million last year. Zedge ended the quarter with $18.5 million in cash and cash equivalents and no debt.

Q&A

  • Derek Greenberg, Maxim Group LLC, Research Division: "My first question is just on the DataSeeds offering. I was wondering if you could help us understand how this gets recognized as revenue in terms of what line item that shows up in? And then just the structure of -- if you receive an order, is that recognized within that quarter or is it recognized over time? Or just how to think about these things?" Yi Tsai replied, "DataSeeds, when we receive the order, we start production, we only recognize the revenue when the products are delivered and accepted. So it could be across quarter. For the time being, because the revenue is still insignificant, we just group it under digital goods and services, the same line as GuruShots legacy business."

  • Greenberg: "Could you talk a little bit more about just GuruShots and how that product is tied to DataSeeds?" CEO Reich explained, "We have delivered content from both the existing catalog where users have opted in to have their content used for AI training as well as from launching competitions specific to meeting the prospects content needs. ... We have not made a decision as of yet [on GuruShots 2.0 direction]."

  • Greenberg also asked about Emojipedia's outlook. Reich responded, "I can't answer that as of now. I think we're going to need another quarter to see how Google continues to advance with the copy-paste feature and what's happening with AI overviews."

  • On restructuring costs, Tsai specified, "At the end of July, we booked somewhere around $330,000 withholding taxes, which was paid out in October, and that impacted our free cash flow during Q1 '26."

  • Greenberg asked about remaining authorization for share buybacks. Tsai answered, "We have $600,000 left under the current $5 million buyback authorization."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on details of DataSeeds revenue recognition, GuruShots and Emojipedia performance, and clarity on restructuring costs, with a tone that was neutral and information-seeking.

  • Management maintained a confident and forward-looking tone in prepared remarks, frequently referencing momentum, operational discipline, and the ability to offset headwinds. In the Q&A, Reich and Tsai were measured, transparent about uncertainties, particularly regarding Emojipedia and GuruShots, and clear about the early stage of DataSeeds.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone remained consistent, with management displaying slightly more operational confidence due to progress in subscriptions and the DataSeeds pipeline.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language maintained a focus on growth, innovation, and cost discipline. Management continued to express confidence in the business model and the momentum in subscription and DataSeeds performance.

  • Strategic focus shifted slightly, with more emphasis on the scaling potential of DataSeeds and continued innovation pipeline development.

  • Analysts continued to probe for clarity on business segment performance and the impact of restructuring, with questions shifting toward the specifics of new business lines and their operational impact.

  • Key metric changes included a rise in active subscriptions to 1.1 million, increased deferred revenue, and improved GAAP profitability.

  • Management's confidence in their innovation and cost management initiatives appeared strengthened, while acknowledging ongoing uncertainties in segments like Emojipedia and GuruShots.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted anticipated declines at Emojipedia and GuruShots, but emphasized that "strength across the rest of our ecosystem more than offset those headwinds." Reich acknowledged, "I can't answer that as of now" regarding the future of Emojipedia, citing uncertainty about Google's feature changes and industry compensation mechanisms.

  • Tsai disclosed that GuruShots remains unprofitable, with operating losses around $150,000 per month, but expressed hope for breakeven as the product evolves.

  • Temporary free cash flow pressure was attributed to restructuring-related tax payments, which are not expected to repeat going forward.

Final Takeaway

Zedge entered fiscal 2026 with a stronger subscription engine, growing enterprise opportunities through DataSeeds, and a disciplined innovation pipeline. Management underscored the resilience of the core business, the ability to reinvest restructuring savings into high-potential initiatives, and confidence in the company’s long-term value creation strategy, while acknowledging ongoing uncertainties in select business segments and a commitment to capital returns for shareholders.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Google integrates Gemini into its language translation service
data.description:
Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) said Friday its language translation feature will now be powered by its AI model, Gemini.

Google Translate will now be able to leverage Gemini's capabilities to improve translations of phrases with more nuanced meanings, like idioms, local expressions, or slang, the tech giant said in a blog post.

Starting today, the update is rolling out on iOS, Android, and on the web in the U.S. and India, translating between English and nearly 20 languages, including Spanish, Hindi, Chinese, Japanese, and German.

The company is also rolling out a new beta version of live translation that will bring real-time, natural-sounding translations with the help of Gemini’s new, native speech-to-speech translation capabilities.

The beta will roll out on Android in the U.S., Mexico, and India and will support more than 70 languages. Beta for iOS will be available in 2026.

Google Translate is also adding more tools so that users can refine their language skills and track their learning goals. The company is expanding the capability to nearly 20 new countries, including Germany, India, Sweden, and Taiwan.

Source: Blog

data.title: BrightSpring upgraded at KeyBanc on specialty pharmacy and EBITDA prospects
data.description:
KeyBanc Capital Markets has upgraded BrightSpring Health Services (BTSG) to Overweight from Sector Weight, citing the competitive standing of its Specialty Pharmacy network and the sustainability of its EBITDA growth.

BrightSpring’s (BTSG) Onco360 Specialty Pharmacy is positioned to continue expanding the company’s Limited Distribution Drug ((LDD)) portfolio, analyst Matthew Gillmor argued.

The Kentucky-based healthcare services platform has improved the unit’s internal processes over the past 10 years, leading to best-in-class service delivery, he added.

“With $90B of new oncology drugs coming to market by ~2032, we think it seems plausible that BTSG could capture ~$15B, which would support significant top-line growth for many years to come,” the analyst wrote.

While LDDs support topline growth in BTSG’s Specialty Pharmacy, the generic conversions are expected to be a tailwind for the company’s gross profit/EBITDA growth in 2026 and beyond, according to Gillmor.

He also cited BrightSpring’s (BTSG) pending acquisition of 127 home health locations from UnitedHealth (UNH) as part of the managed care giant’s settlement with the DoJ for its acquisition of home health services provider Amedisys.

“We believe this could be a source of underappreciated EBITDA upside for 2026+,” the analyst wrote with a $45 price target on the stock, adding that the deal could be “immediately accretive to BTSG's EV/EBITDA valuation by 2-3%+.”

data.title: Johnson Outdoors outlines cost savings and innovation focus for 2026 amid continued market uncertainty
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Johnson Outdoors (JOUT) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Helen Johnson-Leipold reported, "After a slow start to the beginning of the year, new product successes drove double-digit growth in the second half of the year, resulting in a solid finish to fiscal 2025." She indicated total company sales for the full fiscal year were flat compared to the prior year and noted that operating loss of $16.2 million improved compared to fiscal 2024. Johnson-Leipold highlighted, "Demand exceeded expectations for Humminbird's new XPLORE Series and MEGA Live 2 fishfinders," and mentioned the XPLORE winning "best in category marine electronics honors at ICAST this summer" and receiving the Anglers' Choice Award. She also addressed cost savings, saying, "Our cost savings program remains a priority company-wide, and we continue to work on driving optimal product costs and enhancing operating efficiencies. Cost savings will continue to be a key priority in fiscal 2026."
  • Johnson-Leipold described strong demand for Jetboil's new cooking systems and positive momentum for Old Town's fishing kayak line, while the Camping and Watercraft segment saw a sales decline due to the prior closeout of Eureka! She added, "Excluding the impact of Eureka! sales in the prior year, this segment grew by 2%."
  • In Diving, SCUBAPRO's new Hydros Pro 2 buoyancy control device was recently launched, and Johnson-Leipold said, "We've seen great reception so far with lots of enthusiasm at DEMA, the world's largest scuba diving trade show."
  • On digital and e-commerce, Johnson-Leipold stated, "Our efforts to expand our digital footprint are already fueling growth, and we're excited about the progress."
  • CFO David Johnson provided, "Loss before income taxes for 2025 was $9.3 million compared to a pretax loss of $29.9 million in fiscal 2024, with the improvement mainly due to the $11.2 million write-off of goodwill in the prior year as well as an increase in gross margin and decrease in operating expenses versus the prior year."
  • Johnson highlighted, "Gross margin for the fiscal '25 improved to 35.1%, up 1.2 points from the prior year. We're pleased with our progress on cost savings initiatives, which offset increases in material costs."

Outlook

  • Johnson-Leipold stated, "While it's still too early to tell if the outdoor recreation marketplace has turned the corner, we do expect global macroeconomic challenges to continue to drive uncertainties and our strategic priorities remain more important than ever. Heading into fiscal 2026, we feel confident that our ongoing investment in the consumer-driven innovation and digital and e-commerce excellence, along with our continued hard work on operational efficiencies are the right drivers to position Johnson Outdoors for future success."
  • Johnson said, "Cost savings will continue to be a key priority in fiscal 2026."

Financial Results

  • Johnson reported, "Loss before income taxes for 2025 was $9.3 million compared to a pretax loss of $29.9 million in fiscal 2024."
  • A tax expense of $25 million was recorded, driven by a $25.9 million noncash reserve on U.S. deferred tax assets.
  • Johnson stated, "Gross margin for the fiscal '25 improved to 35.1%, up 1.2 points from the prior year."
  • Operating expenses decreased by 8% or $20.2 million from the prior fiscal year.
  • Johnson said, "Our inventory balance was at the end of the year was $170.7 million, down about $39 million from fiscal '24."
  • The company remains debt-free with a healthy cash position.

Q&A

  • Anthony Lebiedzinski, Sidoti & Company, LLC: "...the strong year-over-year sales gain in the fourth quarter along with the gross margin improvements. So as we think about the fourth quarter revenue gain...have you seen this momentum continue into early fiscal '26?" Johnson-Leipold: "We were really excited about what happened in the third and fourth quarter...so far, at least it's just very early in the year, but...the market momentum is continuing as far as we can see. But there's no -- we're not ready to say the market has turned the corner."
  • Lebiedzinski: "...the pipeline coming up for '26...anything you can just talk at a high level as far as new product pipeline for next year?" Johnson-Leipold: "We're focused on building the pipeline across every business...innovation is our key priority, and we will always focus on that."
  • Lebiedzinski: "...pricing actions...what's been the reception from the retail partners?" Johnson: "We did take pricing where it made sense...the retailers understand our trade partners understand it. It hasn't affected the business right now."
  • Lebiedzinski: "...operational efficiencies and enhancing your manufacturing processes...how much it helps your gross margin?" Johnson: "It was over 1 point of gross margin that we drove to the bottom line through the efforts. And we've got a full portfolio of cost savings initiatives going into fiscal '26."
  • Lebiedzinski: "...how do we think about the effective tax rate for fiscal '26?" Johnson: "With the reserve in place, we would expect the tax rate going forward to be normal in a more normal range. So mid- to high 20s, something like that."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed a neutral to slightly positive tone, highlighting strong sales and gross margin improvements but probing for sustainability and forward momentum. Questions centered on demand continuation, pipeline strength, pricing actions, and cost savings impact.
  • Management maintained a cautiously optimistic tone in prepared remarks and Q&A, emphasizing progress and momentum but reiterating market uncertainty. Johnson-Leipold stated, "...we're not ready to say the market has turned the corner," and Johnson repeatedly underscored ongoing cost savings and operational focus.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone remains cautiously optimistic, with both analysts and management attentive to market volatility but recognizing improved performance in the second half.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Both quarters featured innovation and cost control as strategic priorities, but the fourth quarter saw greater emphasis on digital and e-commerce growth.
  • Management's confidence was bolstered by stronger second-half results, yet guidance language remained cautious given persistent market uncertainties.
  • Analysts continued to focus on sustainability of improvements, new product pipeline, effect of pricing, and cost structure.
  • Gross margin improvement and further inventory reduction were highlighted in both quarters, with the fourth quarter noting a continued debt-free balance sheet and cash position.
  • Strategic focus shifted slightly toward strengthening digital capabilities alongside ongoing cost savings.

Risks and Concerns

  • Johnson-Leipold cited global macroeconomic challenges and market uncertainty as ongoing risks.
  • Johnson noted that a $25.9 million noncash reserve on U.S. deferred tax assets was established due to recent operating losses.
  • Management stated that tariffs continue to present challenges, but mitigation strategies and pricing actions are being implemented.
  • The company remains focused on operational efficiencies and cost savings to manage risk.

Final Takeaway

Johnson Outdoors closed fiscal 2025 with improved second-half performance driven by new product launches, gross margin gains, and reduced operating expenses. The company remains focused on innovation, digital expansion, and operational efficiency heading into fiscal 2026, while acknowledging ongoing macroeconomic uncertainties and emphasizing continued prudence in cost management and market responsiveness.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Swiss regulator opens probe into whether Apple terms hinder payment app competition
data.description:
The Secretariat of the Swiss Competition Commission opened a preliminary investigation into whether Apple's (AAPL) terms and conditions prevent other providers of mobile payment apps from competing effectively with Apple Pay for contactless payments with iOS devices in shops.

Until 2024, Apple (AAPL) denied third parties access to the near-field communications ("NFC") interface on iOS devices, the operating system that runs on Apple devices. But on July 11, 2024, the European Commission accepted commitments offered by the tech giant to grant third parties free access to the NFC interface on iOS devices and declared them legally binding under EU antitrust law.

As a result of talks between the Secretariat and Apple (AAPL) since early 2024, Apple has granted Swiss third-party app providers access to the NFC and SE platform on iOS devices since late 2024.

"The Secretariat is now conducting a preliminary investigation to determine whether the terms and conditions for granting access – which differ from those applicable in the EEA – comply with Swiss antitrust law. To this end, the Secretariat continues to gather information and insights from the markets," the regulator said.

data.title: Nasdaq slides 1.5% as Broadcom guidance concerns pressures AI trade
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages were lower on Friday, with the Nasdaq Composite retracting the most as the AI trade came under pressure dragging the index.

The teach-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was last -1.4% in afternoon trade, while the S&P 500 (SP500) was -0.9%.

The blue-chip Dow (DJI) had continued making new highs reaching a top of 48,886.86 points, but retrieved and was last -0.3%.

Chipmaker Broadcom (AVGO) -10.2% warned of margin pressures and disappointed investors with a lesser-than-expected backlog figure for AI product orders. 

“The bull is just three years old, there’s still a massive Wall of Worry to climb, there’s plenty of cash on the sidelines, corporate earnings are better than analysts expected, the consumer is not as dire as many proclaimed, and the Fed is easing – not tightening. Plus, many sectors and stocks are well rested and ready to run,” said Robert Edwards, CIO at Edwards Asset Management.

“What a run the mega-cap growth shares have been on – impressive. But we think 2026 is the year the leadership broadens and the baton is passed. Mega-cap growth has carried the load, but about two handfuls of companies now represent nearly 40% of the index. It’s time to spread the money around. Dividend growers, value, industrials, smaller companies – all have been forgotten, and forgotten stocks, whose operations are strong, often provide the biggest surprise,” Edwards added.

Also, among the Nasdaq’s ((COMP:IND)) top decliners are Micron Technology (MU), Applovin (APP), Palantir (PLTR), and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD).

On the economic side, Chicago Federal Reserve President Austan Goolsbee said that waiting on more data before lowering interest rates would have been more prudent. Also, Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack said she would prefer a “slightly more” restrictive monetary policy.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 3 basis points to 4.19%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 3.55%.

data.title: Kalshi brings prediction markets to crypto wallet Phantom's 20M users
data.description:
Kalshi (KALSHI) is bringing its prediction markets to Phantom, a self-custody crypto wallet platform with over 20M users, the companies said Friday.

The integration allows Phantom users to trade on the outcomes of real-world events using Solana-based tokens and CASH, the stablecoin launched by Phantom earlier in the year.

Several third parties recently said they have signed up with Kalshi (KALSHI) and would mint tokens referencing positions held on Kalshi markets, giving users the option to make tokenized trades on the blockchain.

"Phantom opens a major new channel for growth by bringing our markets to millions of crypto-native users who want to express opinions on real-world events,” said Kalshi CEO Tarek Mansour. “At Kalshi, we’re looking to be a leader onchain like we are offchain, and partnering with Phantom is a significant step in helping us achieve that goal."

On Thursday, Kalshi (KALSHI) and Crypto.com announced a coalition for prediction markets, which include Coinbase (COIN), Robinhood (HOOD) and sports gaming platform Underdog.

data.title: CEO exit triggers Wall Street support for lululemon overhaul
data.description:
Wall Street is applauding lululemon athletica’s (LULU) third-quarter results — and, more notably, the announcement that CEO Calvin McDonald will step down after a tenure marked by a tripling of revenue since 2017 but a 45% slide in the stock this year amid rising competition, a strained consumer, and the loss of U.S. shipping exemptions for Canadian imports.

“The news that McDonald plans to step down will be welcomed by the market,” BTIG’s Janine Strichter said on the heels of Thursday’s announcement, acknowledging that "investors were looking for quicker and bigger changes to revive a stagnant Americas business.”

Third quarter results -- although better-than-anticipated -- underscored some of the challenges a new CEO will face: a soft American market, and tariffs that will continue to weigh on the company’s profit margin.

“While we’d expect a pop [in shares] in the near-term with the CEO change a catalyst, we remain cautious given the work that remains,” Wells Fargo’s Ike Boruchow says, also pointing to the sluggish North American market, margin headwinds, and loss of the de minimis import exemption.

For Jefferies’ Randall Konik — a long-time critic of lululemon (LULU) — Calvin McDonald’s departure is the most encouraging development he’s seen for the company.

“Lululemon must return to its roots, and we urge the board to find a leader to do so,” Konik says, highlighting that the brand has been diluted by “inconsistent design and expansion into non-core categories.”

The result, Konik says, is the alienation of loyal customers, mounting competitive pressures, and a business in clear decline.

“The board’s decision to move on is overdue and, in our view, the most constructive development for shareholders in years,” he adds, ditching his long-held Sell rating on lululemon (LULU) for Hold with a 41% bump to his price target to $170.

Needham’s Tom Nikic also sees the need for a new CEO to bring about a “new viewpoint” for the brand, but warns that a CEO transition, “adds to the uncertainty in the name,” with the added weight that C-suite executives usually like to “reset numbers” upon taking the job, raising concerns about lululemon’s (LULU) conservative initial FY26 outlook.

“Management expects the ‘negatives to outweigh the positives’ which suggests pressure will continue next year,” Nikic says in his note to clients.

For the time being, investors are giving lululemon’s (LULU) outlook a thumbs-up with a 10% gain and 4 ½ month high for the stock on Friday.

data.title: Pfizer declares $0.43 dividend
data.description:
• Pfizer (PFE) declares $0.43/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 6.67%
  • Payable March 6; for shareholders of record Jan. 23; ex-div Jan. 23.
  • See PFE Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.
